evidence.correctness_and_freshness: Uses fabricated/incorrect stripe-python API surface: `from stripe import Stripe` and `stripe_client = Stripe(api_key=...)` is not the standard stripe-python pattern (which is `import stripe; stripe.api_key=...`). Methods like `stripe_client.paymentIntents.create` (camelCase), `stripe_client.v2.core.accounts.create`, `stripe_client.v2.core.accountLinks.create`, `webhooks.constructEvent` are JS-style camelCase, not Python snake_case (should be `stripe.PaymentIntent.create`, `stripe.Account.create`, `stripe.AccountLink.create`, `stripe.Webhook.construct_event`). Core integration is essentially fabricated for Python.
evidence.executability: Multiple Python files will fail at import/runtime: `from stripe import Stripe` will ImportError; `Column('metadata', ...)` conflicts with SQLAlchemy reserved attribute name; relative imports `from ..schemas` won't work given the stated structure with main.py at backend root and `from routes import connect`. Final code block is truncated mid-statement (`idempotency_key=idemp`). Webhook testing section requires `brew install` (sandbox-infeasible) and `stripe listen` tunnel-like pattern.
evidence.completeness: (a) API key acquisition present; (b) install bash present (though has invalid `npm install axios==1.6.0` syntax); (c) env template present; (d) backend code present but truncated; (e) frontend code present; (f) test commands present but rely on Stripe CLI. Playbook is truncated at the end — incomplete final code block.
evidence.citation_anchoring: Inline numeric citations [1][3][5] etc. reference an unseen bibliography; no canonical docs.stripe.com URLs anchored near code. Only stripe.com homepage and a stripe-cli docs URL appear in prose.

rubric_notes: docs_unavailable=true; applied long-tail handling — internal inconsistencies (camelCase methods, fabricated Stripe class import, truncated final block) plus no version self-consistency yield CORRECTNESS=1. Truncation also impacts executability/completeness.
